claim: Shell achieved more than 60% of its target to halve Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2030 compared with 2016 levels.
Shell reduced freshwater consumption by 15% compared with 2018 in water-stressed areas.
Shell is working to develop a circular economy for plastics despite regulatory and technological challenges.
Shell invested $5.6 billion in low-carbon energy solutions in 2023.
Shell’s net carbon intensity was 74 gCO2e/MJ in 2023.
Shell reported 63 Tier 1 and Tier 2 operational process safety events in 2023.
Shell experienced 70 operational spills of more than 100 kilograms in 2023.
Shell aims to maintain methane emissions intensity below 0.2% and achieve near-zero methane emissions by 2030.
Shell’s target is to become a net-zero emissions energy business by 2050.
Shell invested 49% of its R&D budget in 2023 on decarbonisation projects.
Shell’s serious injury and fatality frequency was 2.6 per 100 million working hours in 2023.
Shell reduced customer emissions from oil products compared with 2021 levels.
Shell signed the Oil and Gas Decarbonization Charter at COP28.
Shell advocated policies to enable commercial investments in the energy transition
Shell invested one-third of its total capital spending in low-carbon and non-energy products in 2023.
Shell advocated policies to enable commercial investments in the energy transition
In 2023, about 94% of the oil spills of more than 100 kilograms from the SPDC-operated facilities were caused by the illegal activities of third parties.
